header_summary:
The recent discourse highlights a proposed method that enables quantum adversaries to authenticate cryptographic prowess without Zero-Knowledge protocols by computing the discrete logarithm on a cryptographic curve, potentially simplifying authentication processes while mitigating collision risks by fixing variables within the proof system [source](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/zG-JBFlHqnB2ZXTgBqIPn75VUiXkjrvBrO1CMN78gccfE4BqP3LmWnxB0cu2FQItr_cLoDcMFzlWaXkv3xeUDTOZrJgEGDRvWbpvADCBAZo=@proton.me/T/#m116b880136453376b29d79f2cc42b5951d9c6c85). 

Analysis of Bitcoin's evolving address formats reveals slow adoption rates, with data indicating minimal uptake even years after introduction. This trend might reflect the broader challenges of integrating new cryptographic technologies within established systems, despite potential enhancements in security against quantum threats. The community is also contemplating proactive measures to protect against quantum vulnerabilities by potentially deploying post-quantum cryptographic methods in response to significant security breaches [source](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/doSPUSJvChwux9prI1puFj9OMI_ZeCeIEb1KKhnlp6G_wnpkvZmo3FZvvMRvaLAhzQFF2NS1Ad1tHDWfnmf6ytLI7oMotciw64hUZkHoI68=@proton.me/T/#mb375ada540cf9d530c3237dbd1fde1efee285ae7).

In another development, the P2WOTS initiative is gaining attention for its potential to secure Bitcoin transactions against quantum attacks, with extensive testing confirming its robustness. The community is encouraged to participate and provide feedback through interactive platforms and detailed documentation available on the project's GitHub and official demo site [source](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/p2wots-live-signet-interactive-wallet-demo/2557).

recent_posts.title: Weak Quantum Bounty Ceremony
recent_posts.summary: - A novel method proposes quantum adversaries authenticate without Zero-Knowledge protocols.  - The method involves computing discrete logarithms to simplify cryptographic verification.  - It addresses collision risk by fixing variables and suggests quantum-adaptive payment solutions.

recent_posts.title: Aligning privacy incentives in P2MR
recent_posts.summary:
- Historical trends show slow adoption of new Bitcoin formats like P2TR.
- Future strategies may include deploying Post-Quantum cryptographic features.
- Upcoming improvements will cut Schnorr transaction costs, enhancing blockchain efficiency.

recent_posts.title: Testnet 4 difficulty/hashrate
recent_posts.summary: - The current testnet4 mining shows a big gap between reported and real hashing difficulties.  - Nearly 90% of block confirmations on testnet4 stem from network propagation, not mining effort.  - A real-difficulty block balances about 10 to 12 min-difficulty blocks, maintaining network integrity.

recent_posts.title: Quantum Attack Game Theory
recent_posts.summary:
- The analysis focuses on blockchain decision-making involving transaction fees and block mining.
- It highlights complex mathematics in creating formulas for probabilistic evaluations.
- A coupling argument shows isomorphic probability spaces in different mining outcomes.

recent_posts.title: P2WOTS: Live Signet & Interactive Wallet Demo
recent_posts.summary:
- The P2WOTS initiative enhances Bitcoin security against quantum threats through testing and community involvement.
- A user-friendly interface allows interactive proposal testing via a custom signet accessible online.
- Further details and code updates are available on the project's GitHub repository for developer engagement.

recent_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Witness Version 3: ML-DSA-65 Post-Quantum Key-Path Spending
recent_posts.summary:
- The proposal for ML-DSA key commitment lacks cryptographic flexibility and security.
- Utilizing ML-DSA-65 over ML-DSA-44 lacks sufficient justification for increased resource usage.
- Alternatives like SLH-DSA could offer more security and efficiency than ML-DSA.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] P2P UTXO Set Sharing
active_posts.summary: - The new BIP introduces enhancements for decentralized UTXO set sharing in the Bitcoin network.  - It incorporates `NODE_UTXO_SET` service bit and four P2P messages to facilitate data transfer.  - This proposal aims to reduce setup times and improve network resilience and decentralization.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Testnet 5
active_posts.summary:
- A new Testnet5 draft has been proposed to improve on Testnet4 issues.
- The draft focuses on usability and testing BIP54, addressing two main concerns.
- Feedback on the draft can be added on GitHub or discussed on the Bitcoin Mailing List.

active_posts.title: Prohibit Merkle Internal Node Preimages That Encode Minimal 64-Byte Transactions
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal introduces a new rule to enhance Bitcoin's transaction security.
- It targets specific 64-byte transactions to prevent SPV malleability issues.
- A C++ function is included to check for prohibited Merkle node preimages.

active_posts.title: BIP-110 update: v0.4.1 release and implementation submitted to Bitcoin Core
active_posts.summary:
- BIP-110 v0.4.1 focuses on enhancing Bitcoin by restricting transaction data fields to 256 bytes.
- The update, set for potential December 2025 activation, mandates 55% hashpower support.
- Implementation is supported across multiple platforms and has been integrated into Bitcoin Core.

active_posts.title: Default signet reorg history — was the fork at height 287766 (depth 19) deliberate?
active_posts.summary: - A recent fork in Bitcoin's testnet at height 287766 extended 19 blocks.  - Developers lack historical data on similar reorganizations, needing better transparency.  - Establishing if such reorgs are typical will help developers prepare realistic tests.

active_posts.title: Bird of Prey 2: non-malleable Schnorr + PQ signatures
active_posts.summary:
- The `BoP-2` scheme combines Schnorr-like and PQC signatures for improved security.
- It uses hash computations and nonce modifications to ensure signature integrity.
- The approach focuses on security enhancements viable against quantum attacks.

today_in_history_posts.title: UHS: Full-node security without maintaining a full UTXO set
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- The proposal recommends storing only output hashes, reducing pruned node size by 40%.
- Bandwidth could increase by 5%, and transaction inefficiencies may add 25% overhead.
- Testing on Cory's branch is essential to evaluate RAM usage effectiveness on low-end devices.

today_in_history_posts.title: New idea on decentralized identity and truth (Re: Numerifides)
today_in_history_posts.summary: - The author proposed a decentralized identity system using Lightning.  - The system allows nodes to query mappings and answers for a Lightning payment.  - Charges would rise with longer paths or increased queries, costing 1 satoshi per query.

today_in_history_posts.title: Deflationary money is a Good Thing
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Investment strategies differ as either passive or active, with distinctive management levels.
- Passive investment issues include asset underutilization due to misaligned manager incentives.
- Proposed solutions involve central banks stabilizing economies using deflationary currencies like Bitcoin.
